message.content: Adversaries may exploit Microsoft Outlook rules to maintain persistence on compromised systems. Outlook rules, typically used to automate email management, can be manipulated to execute malicious code when a specially crafted email is received. This technique leverages the automatic loading of these rules upon Outlook startup, enabling adversaries to trigger code execution remotely. Tools like Ruler can facilitate the creation and deployment of such malicious rules. The impact includes unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and potential lateral movement within the network. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring and auditing Outlook rules, restricting the ability to create or modify rules, and employing email filtering to detect and block suspicious emails. Regularly updating and patching email clients and enforcing multi-factor authentication can further reduce the risk.
